purpose: The commit fixes a bug where commands could be queued after device unregistration, leading to use-after-free issues.
Removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c to simplify dependencies.
Updates documentation for 'N/A' store metric in perf c2c, clarifying its meaning and usage.
Fixes race conditions and synchronization issues in hugetlb vma lock handling during split and unmapping.
Adds AVDD and MICVDD supply documentation to device tree bindings for Realtek rt5682s codec.
Fixes a resource leak by adding missing iounmap() call in remove function.
Removes an unused work_struct from mlx5e_async_ctx struct.
Changes key_idx type from unsigned to signed to prevent static checker warnings when -1 is assigned.
This commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfig files without introducing functional changes.
Refactors code by replacing open-coded checks with a helper function to improve readability and maintainability.
The commit modifies self-tests to skip RTC tests when the hardware is not present, preventing false failures.
Removes redundant assignment of variable 'bytes' which was unused and caused a dead store warning.
Decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks to reduce kernel size and complexity.
Removes a duplicated word 'be split' in a comment within spte.h, improving code readability.
Reverts a change causing incorrect clock table population to fix a bug leading to underflow.
Adds support for Renesas RZ/V2L SoC with Mali-G31 GPU by updating device tree bindings.
The commit renames a struct to improve code consistency.
Fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting the format specifier in a debug message, improving code correctness.
Fixes a branch range check by casting BIT(25) to long for correct signed comparison.
Removes HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD handling by directly calling the function instead, simplifying the code.
The commit changes a non-global symbol declaration to static, addressing sparse warnings about symbol visibility.
The commit corrects the device tree configuration by removing incorrect interrupt flags on MDSS nodes.
The commit modifies array allocation in struct ctrl_ref, renaming dynamic array handling to a more general approach and preparing for driver-controlled array sizing.
Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs by updating the socinfo driver with new entries.

The commit improves the recognition of all possible chip cuts (versions) for RTL8xxxu WiFi drivers by replacing a switch statement with a direct calculation, enhancing code clarity and maintainability.
Removes custom enable state tracking for PRCMU clocks, relying on core's count instead.
The commit reorganizes and reduces the use of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL by grouping related definitions together.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree to support I2C functionality.
The commit improves code comments and fixes style issues for better clarity.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when failing to acquire hardware semaphore during resource release, especially if hardware is removed unexpectedly.
Updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI terminology by renaming 'master_mode' to 'provider_mode', improving code readability and consistency.
Removes unnecessary IS_SWAPFILE conditional compilation directives as they are always defined and serve no purpose.
Implements GTP echo response functionality to comply with specifications when userspace daemons aren't present.
Replaces cpumask_next with cpumask_first where appropriate and adds cpumask_first_zero function.
Removes an obsolete firmware call causing console errors during roaming.
Adds support for a platform without an amplifier by introducing a new board configuration.
Adds a check to fail zerocopy sendmsg on unsupported sockets, preventing errors.
The commit modifies LaTeX documentation to conditionally load xeCJK.sty only if it exists, preventing build errors on systems without the package.
The commit modifies checkpatch to ignore macros starting with 'case', preventing false positive errors.
Refactor simpledrm to use regular atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers, improving code architecture and maintainability without introducing new features or fixing bugs.
Adds jpeg decode worker interface to manage parallel HW operations on MT8195.
Removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and a function (ata_scsi_dump_cdb) as they are redundant with tracepoints.
Adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods in AMD display driver, optimizing power usage.
Fixes CFI violation by correcting function parameter type from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type.
Corrects the property name from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' in device tree bindings.
The commit suppresses an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by adding a flag to disable it.
Replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify power management code.
Refactor file installation/removal functions to improve organization and readability.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes with independent configurations, improving hardware representation.
Removes IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and integrates its content into IWL_DEVICE_BZ to simplify code structure.
